summary: Iter 17 host-disconnect teardown verified end-to-end. New pytest /app/backend/tests/test_host_disconnect.py exercises real dual-WebSocket flow against the deployed backend and asserts: (a) viewer receives host_left {host_name} then closes 4404 when host WS drops, (b) ROOMS/PENDING/ROOM_STATE/STREAM_STARTS are cleared (proven by host reconnect getting fresh welcome with yt_video_id=null, mode='webrtc' after previously setting yt mode), (c) db.events contains host_left with matching actor_id+room_id, (d) db.rooms doc for the room is preserved (host can reopen), (e) regression: viewer disconnect only fires participant_left and host WS remains alive, (f) frontend WatchRoom.js switch cases 'host_left' and 'room_closed' exist and both navigate to /dashboard. All 3 new tests PASS. Existing test_host_request + test_invites suites still green (15 passed).
action_items: Pre-existing (NOT introduced this iter): /app/backend/tests/test_iter16_no_upload_leak.py uses r.json()['access_token'] but /api/auth/login does not return access_token in the JSON body (only sets it as HttpOnly cookie via _set_cookie). All 3 tests in that file error/fail with KeyError. Main agent should either (i) update _login helper in test_iter16 to pull the cookie via session.cookies.get('access_token'), or (ii) add access_token to UserOut response. Not blocking iter 17 sign-off.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py:1389-1426 finally block correctly resets ROOMS/PENDING/ROOM_STATE/STREAM_STARTS for host disconnect and logs host_left + stream_ended events. Non-host branch preserves room state. Verified live.
server.py:638 _log_event stores under field name event_type (not type) — tests must query accordingly. Consistent with rest of code.
WatchRoom.js:279-286 both host_left and room_closed cases present, both navigate to /dashboard with toasts.
seed_data_creation: Upserted db.room_visits {viewer_user_id, SuperAdmin Room room_id} to guarantee auto-admit (bypasses knock queue) for the viewer WS.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Login endpoint /api/auth/login does NOT return access_token in JSON body — only sets HttpOnly cookie. Extract via session.cookies.get('access_token') or requests.Session cookiejar. For WS connect: wss://{host}/api/ws/room/{room_id}?token=<access_token>. _log_event writes field 'event_type' not 'type' in db.events. To bypass knock queue for viewer WS auto-admit, upsert db.room_visits {user_id, room_id}.
